hpi_details: substernal, 7/10, radiates to left arm, onset at rest, no relief with antacids
concerns: afraid of heart attack — father died of MI at 62
expectations: wants reassurance and something for the pain
pmh: hypertension x5 years, type 2 diabetes x3 years

reference_soap.subjective: 58M presenting with 2hr substernal chest pain 7/10, radiating to left arm, onset at rest. Denies relief with antacids. PMH: HTN, T2DM. Smokes 20 pack-years.
reference_soap.assessment: ACS until proven otherwise. DDx: STEMI, NSTEMI, unstable angina, aortic dissection, PE.
reference_soap.plan: ECG, troponin x2, CXR, aspirin 325mg, IV access, O2, cardiology consult, NPO.

learning_objectives: Elicit cardiac history systematically using SOCRATES
Explore cardiac risk factors: HTN, DM, smoking, family Hx, dyslipidaemia
Address patient's ICE — especially fear of MI
Verbalize ACS differential and dangerous diagnosis first
